questions: Use this page only to locate lessons.
teacher_prompt.scaffold: Point or gesture; offer two choices; model one word or phrase; invite repetition.

parent_prompt.conversation: Not applicable as a visible child task. The adult may use the page only for its stated navigation, publication, or completion purpose.
parent_prompt.home_activity: No additional home-learning activity is added.

illustration.style: Premium preschool workbook; friendly 2D vector-like forms; clean white base; soft pastel accents; thick rounded outlines.
negative_constraints: Do not redraw, imitate, replace, distort, crop, or generate the BCube logo; reserve the specified header position for the official vector asset.
Do not add unapproved text, labels, activities, frames, balloons, answer spaces, decorations, watermarks, QR codes, or third-party marks.
Do not exceed two core child activities plus one optional extension; retain the page-specific lower limit when it is stricter.
Do not use dark full-page backgrounds, gradients that impair legibility, visual clutter, tiny objects, thin faint outlines, or unsafe edge placement.
Do not introduce frightening imagery, stereotypes, shame, competitive ranking, inaccessible instructions, or developmentally inappropriate writing load.
Do not alter Star's approved identity, colours, proportions, costume, expression language, or role.
Do not leave unexplained blank space: every box, line, card, balloon, frame, and open area must serve the named learning action.
Do not output mockups, perspective views, device screens, page curls, hands holding the page, crop marks inside trim, or multiple page variants.
No placeholder modules, placeholder page numbers, or worksheet activities.
illustration.illustrator_notes: Use editable page ranges and module names. Individual execution locked: 0 child activities; No blank response boxes.
illustration.approved_source_blueprint: INDIVIDUAL PAGE EXECUTION BLUEPRINT — PAGE 3: Learning objective: Help parents and teachers navigate the workbook. Primary child activity: Show the exact final module names and verified page ranges. Secondary activity: Use one simple icon per module. Teacher/parent prompt: Use this page only to locate lessons. Expected child response: No child response required. Blank-space allocation: No blank response boxes. Activity load: 0 child activities. Prohibited extras: No placeholder modules, placeholder page numbers, or worksheet activities. Every box, balloon, line, card, and frame must have a stated learning purpose. Do not invent additional activities or unexplained blank areas.

compiled_prompt:
# BCube Production Prompt v3 — CC-NUR-V3-P003

## 1. Release metadata

- Prompt ID: CC-NUR-V3-P003
- Prompt package version: 3.0.0
- Compiler: BCube Prompt Compiler 3.0.0
- Release date: 2026-07-15
- Book: Communication Champions — Gold Edition
- Level: Nursery (3+)
- Module: Front Matter
- Page: 3 of 41
- Exact page title: Contents
- Source status: FINAL
- Source workbook SHA-256: caecd24a409b4f531b6018b2076d42cc9222af2f198e7c9e5835cde0bc54bfba

## 2. Production command

Create exactly one final, flat, front-facing A4 portrait workbook page for page 3, “Contents”. Treat this package as a deterministic production specification for an illustrator, layout artist, or image-generation workflow. Preserve all approved page-specific instructions. Do not invent content. Produce only this page; do not produce explanations or alternates.

## 3. Compiler order and precedence

- 1. Publishing Engine
- 2. Design Engine
- 3. Visual Grammar Engine
- 4. Educational Engine
- 5. Teaching Engine
- 6. Parent Partnership Engine
- 7. Illustration Engine
- 8. Character Engine
- 9. Quality Assurance and Prompt Compiler

Apply rules in this precedence: approved page-specific blueprint > approved unit/book rule > global rule. An override is valid only when explicitly stated in this package. Resolve no conflict by guessing; stop and flag it for editorial review.

## 4. Publishing Engine

- Format: A4 portrait, 210 × 297 mm.
- Production resolution: 300 DPI, print-ready; CMYK-safe colour decisions; 3 mm bleed; keep essential content at least 10 mm inside trim.
- Header/title: Use a 20–28 mm header zone. Reserve top-left placement for the official BCube Future Academy vector logo and centre the exact page title “Contents”. Never generate or redraw the logo.
- Footer: Use a 10–14 mm footer zone and show page number 3 only at bottom right; no extra footer copy.
- Cover-only age badge rule: Use Nursery (3+) on the cover only; do not repeat it on interior pages.
- Output: one print-ready page with clean trim, bleed, safe-zone discipline, and editable text/logo layers in final assembly.

## 5. Design Engine

- Page type: Front Matter.
- Activity/layout requirement: Two-column navigation layout.
- Activity load: 0 child activities.
- Blank-space allocation: No blank response boxes.
- Use a clear top-to-bottom hierarchy: identity/title, one dominant learning focus, child action area, compact adult support when required, then footer.
- Background and palette: Clean white with soft pastel accents; no dark full-page backgrounds; Soft peach, mint, lavender, sky blue, sunshine yellow; limited bright accents.
- Typography and marks: large, highly legible early-years type; thick rounded outlines; generous spacing; no essential information carried by colour alone.

## 6. Visual Grammar Engine

- Establish one unmistakable focal point directly serving “Contents”.
- Guide the eye from title to model/example to child action to supported response.
- Use repetition only to clarify sequence, comparison, choice, or turn-taking.
- Keep cognitive load appropriate for age 3+: large targets, few choices, clear grouping, obvious action affordances, and no decorative competition with the task.
- Every visual element must support the learning objective or production identity.

## 7. Educational Engine

- Learning objective: Help parents and teachers navigate the workbook.
- Primary child activity: Show the exact final module names and verified page ranges.
- Secondary speaking/reflection: Use one simple icon per module.
- Observable evidence: No child response required.
- Use inclusive, familiar, concrete contexts. Accept speech, pointing, gesture, imitation, drawing, colouring, matching, or adult-supported dictation exactly where the blueprint permits.

## 8. Teaching Engine

- Facilitation: Use this page only to locate lessons.
- Model first, invite one child turn, pause for processing, then affirm the attempt without correcting through shame.
- Approved question: Use this page only to locate lessons.
- Expected response: No child response required.
- Scaffold in this order: point/gesture → offer two choices → model one word or phrase → invite repetition. Do not force a longer response.

## 9. Parent Partnership Engine

- Conversation prompt: Not applicable as a visible child task. The adult may use the page only for its stated navigation, publication, or completion purpose.
- Home reinforcement: No additional home-learning activity is added.
- Use common household materials only; no purchase, printing, device, or specialist knowledge is required.
- The parent prompt supports the page objective and must not create a new scored activity.

## 10. Illustration Engine

- Rendering style: premium preschool workbook illustration; friendly 2D vector-like forms; clean white base; soft pastel accents; thick, clean, rounded black outlines; large enclosed colouring areas where applicable.
- Scene/layout anchor: Two-column navigation layout.
- Page-specific composition: Build one coherent front matter composition for “Contents”. Visually demonstrate: Show the exact final module names and verified page ranges. Support it with: Use one simple icon per module. Preserve the response evidence: No child response required.
- Illustrator notes: Use editable page ranges and module names. Individual execution locked: 0 child activities; No blank response boxes.
- Ensure natural expressions, correct anatomy, age-appropriate proportions, inclusive representation, clear object separation, and unobstructed response spaces.
- Keep all visible copy exact, editable, correctly spelled, and inside safe zones.

## 11. Character Engine

- Star mascot use: Small optional footer placement.
- Use only the locked Star identity: bright yellow rounded five-point star body; warm expressive face; blue shoes; blue cape when the approved pose requires it; friendly preschool proportions; consistent scale, colours, and facial language.
- Star demonstrates, encourages, points, listens, or celebrates without completing the child's answer space.
- Children and adults must show respectful interaction, culturally inclusive presentation, natural pose logic, and clear conversational turn-taking.

## 12. Approved page-specific Gold execution blueprint

INDIVIDUAL PAGE EXECUTION BLUEPRINT — PAGE 3: Learning objective: Help parents and teachers navigate the workbook. Primary child activity: Show the exact final module names and verified page ranges. Secondary activity: Use one simple icon per module. Teacher/parent prompt: Use this page only to locate lessons. Expected child response: No child response required. Blank-space allocation: No blank response boxes. Activity load: 0 child activities. Prohibited extras: No placeholder modules, placeholder page numbers, or worksheet activities. Every box, balloon, line, card, and frame must have a stated learning purpose. Do not invent additional activities or unexplained blank areas.

## 13. Negative constraints

- Do not redraw, imitate, replace, distort, crop, or generate the BCube logo; reserve the specified header position for the official vector asset.
- Do not add unapproved text, labels, activities, frames, balloons, answer spaces, decorations, watermarks, QR codes, or third-party marks.
- Do not exceed two core child activities plus one optional extension; retain the page-specific lower limit when it is stricter.
- Do not use dark full-page backgrounds, gradients that impair legibility, visual clutter, tiny objects, thin faint outlines, or unsafe edge placement.
- Do not introduce frightening imagery, stereotypes, shame, competitive ranking, inaccessible instructions, or developmentally inappropriate writing load.
- Do not alter Star's approved identity, colours, proportions, costume, expression language, or role.
- Do not leave unexplained blank space: every box, line, card, balloon, frame, and open area must serve the named learning action.
- Do not output mockups, perspective views, device screens, page curls, hands holding the page, crop marks inside trim, or multiple page variants.
- No placeholder modules, placeholder page numbers, or worksheet activities.

## 14. Gold QA acceptance gate

Before release, verify: exact page identity; A4 geometry; 3 mm bleed; 10 mm safe margin; official logo reservation; exact title; interior page number where applicable; one learning objective; blueprint-matched activity load; meaningful blank space; teacher/parent facilitation; expected response; mascot continuity; inclusive character quality; print suitability; no prohibited extras; no unresolved variables. Any critical failure blocks release.

## 15. Compiler footer

Prompt ID CC-NUR-V3-P003 | v3.0.0 | source page 3/41 | precedence page > unit/book > global | Gold gate 100/100 | critical defects 0 | status GOLD CERTIFIED.
